engine: R7b credibility: metric half-life (the freshness dial)
method: Same-player cross-season persistence r(delta) = r0 * 0.5^(delta / H), fit log-linearly with a FREE r0. Each batter/bowler-season is reliability pre-shrunk toward the metric's population mean with the stat's own M (n / (n + M)) before the Pearson correlation, and a season gap enters the fit only with >= 300 pooled pairs and a positive r. Forcing r0 = 1 (the naive fit) ignores that sampling noise attenuates r(1) and badly underestimates H, so the honest free-r0 recount is what ships. Each half-life is named to its metric; there is no single global half-life.

boundary_pct.gloss: how fast a batter's boundary-hitting edge goes stale
boundary_pct.unit: share of faced balls hit for four or six

bowling_economy.gloss: how fast a bowler's economy edge goes stale

srplus.gloss: how fast a batter's era-fair strike-rate edge goes stale
srplus.unit: SR+ (era-fair strike rate; 100 = league average that season)
